scenario: Ridge Bank classifies 15000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8 with FEEL. A FEEL output mapping must return the string "HIGH" when the variable amountCents exceeds 1000000 and otherwise return the string "LOW" as a single value used by a downstream decision table.
question: Which FEEL expression implements this conditional in Camunda 8 FEEL?

options.text: if amountCents > 1000000 then "HIGH" else "LOW" uses the FEEL conditional expression keyword form with the documented syntax
amountCents > 1000000 ? "HIGH" : "LOW" uses the ternary conditional operator supported in Camunda 8 FEEL on every expression position
case when amountCents > 1000000 then "HIGH" else "LOW" end uses the FEEL case keyword form with explicit when and end markers in a block
switch(amountCents > 1000000, "HIGH", "LOW") uses the FEEL built-in switch helper that takes a boolean and two branch values as positional arguments

a.text: Correct. FEEL conditional expressions use the if-then-else keyword form.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL does not include the C-style ternary operator.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL does not include a case-when-then-end statement form.

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL has no built-in switch helper of that signature.

explanation: FEEL conditional expressions are written as if <cond> then <a> else <b> using keywords.
